headline: The uncertain fate of non-Russian prisoners of war captured by Ukrainian forces
description: Hundreds of foreign fighters held in captivity in Ukraine find themselves in legal limbo, as Moscow refuses to include them in prisoner exchanges.

headline: Iran says Strait of Hormuz closed again after Israel strikes Lebanon
France issues red heatwave alert on Sunday for third of country, bans public alcohol consumption at music festival
French presidential candidates capitalize on social media, bypassing journalists
description: The rise of social media and digital platforms offers presidential hopefuls new communication channels to reach the French public. This comes at the expense of the media, which must increasingly bow to the conditions imposed by politicians.

headline: German government stokes up the memory wars surrounding the 'displaced persons' of 1945
description: Germany is marking World Refugee Day on Saturday, commemorating the 12 million Germans expelled from Central and Eastern Europe after World War II. Under pressure from the far right, the Christian Democratic government has taken up the issue and chosen to assign it to the Ministry of the Interior rather than the Ministry of Culture.

headline: Does the decline in reading and the rise of screens signal the end of the democratic era?
description: While concerns about the weakening of the mind in the face of new technologies are nothing new, the digital revolution and artificial intelligence now point to a possible end of book civilization.

headline: Is air conditioning essential for coping with heatwaves? France might be changing its mind on the issue
description: While more and more people are installing air conditioning, this technology is still seen by many as a misguided response to climate change. Some experts have challenged that argument as France faces unusually high temperatures.

headline: Thomas Piketty: 'To clarify its own stance and unite its ranks, the left must propose a referendum to adopt a national solidarity tax'

Le Monde's point-by-point breakdown of the fragile US-Iran framework agreement
description: After more than 100 days of war, the United States and Iran signed a memorandum of understanding aimed at ending their conflict. The document will be followed by negotiations to address issues it does not resolve. Le Monde publishes the full text, along with context and analysis.

headline: French environment minister's absence from public debate underscores government's climate inaction
description: Appiointed in October 2025, Monique Barbut is struggling to make her mark. This situation goes beyond her as an individual and highlights how the government has abandoned the issue.

headline: Mozart manuscript discovered at French National Library to be performed for the first time
description: The music notebook, found in the archives, dates from the composer's final stay in Paris, in 1778. The seven short pieces for flute and harp it contains will be broadcast for the first time ever on June 22 on France Musique radio.
